mission.title: litmus: Unable to add tags to an existing experiment
mission.description: Unable to add tags to an existing experiment. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify litmus troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your litmus deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litmus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litmus
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litmus -n litmus --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review litmus configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litmus configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litmus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litmus
kubectl get configmap -n litmus -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litmus
```
**What happened**:

After successful creation of experiment through ChaosCenter UI, if you press edit, go to overview , add tags and try to save the experiment you get error `experiment name should be unique, duplicate experiment found with name:

steps.title: Apply the fix for Unable to add tags to an existing experiment
steps.description:
Refactored the variable `Name` to `ExperimentName`.

There was a bug which was caused because of using wrong variable name.
`Name` was used in place of `ExperimentName` which was causing to trigger the `validateDuplicateExperimentName` even when we edit experiment details.

steps.title: Confirm Unable to add tags to an existing experiment is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litmus -n litmus --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n litmus --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Refactored the variable `Name` to `ExperimentName`.

There was a bug which was caused because of using wrong variable name.
`Name` was used in place of `ExperimentName` which was causing to trigger the `validateDuplicateExperimentName` even when we edit experiment details.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litmus installed or the issue environment reproducible.
